pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Mustard Boy and The Backpass Assassin go head-to-head: a comedy of errors in managerial ineptitude.** England vs Ghana in Foxborough is really an exhibition in which clueless managing gets exposed. Gayden, also known as "The Backpass Assassin," has somehow managed to draft a team led by England, notorious for their quarter-final write-offs yet he sits in dead last with only 13 points—pathetic. Zach, aka "Mustard Boy," picked the likes of Saudi Arabia from tier 4, demonstrating all the foresight of a goldfish; you wonder if he's just listing names from FIFA video games. If you ever needed proof these guys don't belong in a World Cup pool, this match is it. England has been unpredictable at best, and Ghana is no slouch, especially when their midfield decides to show up. But mark my words, Gayden's gonna bungle this one too—it's an art he's mastered. I'll call it: a tight Ghana win purely out of spite for the managers' battlefield of amateur choices.

pundit_takes.subtitle: Sources say Gunner now considering a victory parade for himself.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's England Poised for Redemption Against Zach's Ghana Squad

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Germany Team Brings Hope After Son's Drafting Fiasco
pundit_takes.subtitle: Rafe allegedly considers replacing son's draft picks with a dartboard.
